shape model output for Neptune's 1-bar isobaric surface. models are described in Mankovich et al. 2026, PSJ.
the models summarized in this file assume the symmetric Voyager wind profile from Tollefson et al. (2018, Icarus 311) Table 1.
the distribution of models in this file comes from Markov chain Monte Carlo fits to the Voyager 2 radio occultation reported in Lindal (1992, AJ 103).
these solutions vary the polar radius to fit the Voyager 2 radio occultation to a Gaussian with standard deviation 5 km.
the wind fit polynomial coefficients are also allowed to vary within their Gaussian uncertainties following T18 Table 1.
this model has wind polynomial cofficients a, b, c:    -3.973912e+02    1.999269e-01   -7.985523e-06
mu=sin(phi), phi [radians], and lat_deg [degrees] refer to planetocentric latitude; lat_pg_deg gives planetographic latitude in degrees.
